sections.content:
Comparing before and after, I see the only change is in node 29 (the bottom bar):
- Before: G 64 pixels [[63, 0], [63, 63]]
- After: G 63 pixels [[63, 0], [63, 62]] + new node 30 c 1 [[63, 63]]
So clicking on the cross at (52, 10) only changed the bottom bar (timer). The cross itself didn't changed anything else. This means the bottom bar is just a timer that decreases with each action. The click didn't do anything meaningful to the game state.
Let me think about what the game might be. Looking at the objects:
- Two crosses (one at ~row 10, col 52 and one at ~row 52, col 10)
- Two gray containers with compartments holding shapes:
  - Top container (gray container with green "C" and "E" shapes
  - Bottom container with yellow "n" and "U" shapes
- A yellow rectangle with a dark red pixel
- A green rectangle with a dark red pixel
- Two crosses made of dark red pixels and a black pixel in the center
- A yellow rectangle with a dark red pixel
The bottom bar is a timer that counts down. The crosses are likely represent something to interact with. Let me try clicking on different targets. Maybe I need to click on the containers or the shapes inside them.
Let me try clicking on the green rectangle (node 2) at row 10, col 30 or the yellow rectangle (node 16) at row 31, col 10. Or maybe the crosses need to click on the gray containers (nodes 9 and 18).
Let me try clicking on the green rectangle with the red pixel.
